_comment: Cartopian PM containment — Tier-2 native-sandbox DEPTH profile for the codex harness (FR-007 / TASK-03-001). The Cartopian source of truth, loaded by wrappers/bin/cartopian-codex-pm, that drives codex's OWN native OS sandbox BENEATH the DEC-001 tool-removal floor. codex's native sandbox is Apple Seatbelt (sandbox-exec) on macOS and Landlock+seccomp on Linux, selected by `codex -s <sandbox_mode>` and configured through codex's own config keys (no third-party / bundled sandbox — NF-001). The floor (features.shell_tool=false + features.unified_exec=false) removes the shell/exec tools so this layer is normally inert; it exists for defense-in-depth: if the floor were ever bypassed and a shell/apply_patch tool reappeared, codex's native sandbox still denies the product-repo / work-root WRITE reach, process-exec effects, and network. Absolute paths are host/project-specific, exactly as wrappers/etc/mcp-cartopian-only.json hard-codes the cartopian-mcp path; regenerate them for another project root / work root.
mechanism: codex native sandbox: Apple Seatbelt (sandbox-exec) on macOS, Landlock+seccomp on Linux. Selected by `codex -s <sandbox_mode>`; the standalone `codex sandbox` subcommand runs the same Seatbelt/Landlock policy. Config + native mechanism only; no bundled sandbox (NF-001).

codex_config._note: The exact codex config the floor wrapper applies for the depth layer. `sandbox_mode=read-only` denies ALL filesystem writes (a superset of denying the product-repo / work-root write roots above) and network, and permits no un-sandboxed process exec. `approval_policy=never` closes codex's escalation escape hatch: a sandbox-denied command is returned to the model as a failure and is NEVER escalated out of the sandbox for approval (the model cannot request `require_escalated` execution).

read_denial_note: FORCING RESIDUAL (F1 — read denial is NOT achieved on codex). codex's read-only / workspace-write sandbox modes grant universal READ at the OS layer (no OS-level deny-read of subpaths), AND codex always exposes the BUILT-IN list_mcp_resources / read_mcp_resource tools whenever any configured MCP server advertises the `resources` capability — which the Cartopian server does. There is NO codex-side config or feature flag to suppress those built-ins (they are not in `codex features list`; per-server enabled_tools/disabled_tools only filter the server's own tools, not codex built-ins). Removing the shell/exec tools at the Tier-1 floor therefore does NOT yield a no-read-tool state: a contained codex PM can still read every registered project's Cartopian-mediated REQUIREMENTS / STATE / IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN resource (a cross-project read surface absent from the Claude --tools "" floor; captured in tests/wrappers/pm-codex/evidence/green-03-read). The work-root *filesystem* is NOT reachable (read_mcp_resource reads mediated resources, not arbitrary files). This residual cannot be closed by codex-side assets alone; closing it requires the shared Cartopian MCP server to stop advertising `resources` under contained mode (CARTOPIAN_PM_CONTAINED=1) — a shared-logic change deferred as out of TASK-03-001's scope. Pending that change codex is recorded not-recommended-as-PM-host (forcing residual captured in tests/wrappers/pm-codex/evidence/green-03-read). This depth layer still denies all WRITES, process-exec effects, and network beneath the floor.
web_denial_note: FORCING RESIDUAL (F1b — web/browse denial is NOT achieved on codex). `deny_network` here constrains the per-command Seatbelt/Landlock sandbox, but codex's native `web_search` is a SERVER-SIDE Responses tool — the fetch runs on the provider's servers, not the local sandboxed process — so this OS-level network deny CANNOT block it. It is also not reliably suppressed by config: the bare boolean `tools.web_search=false` is a silently-ignored type mismatch, and even the correct table form `[tools.web_search] disabled=true` (best-effort, applied by the floor) did not reliably remove the tool across repeated captures (tests/wrappers/pm-codex/evidence/green-05-web). A contained codex PM therefore retains a web/browse + data-exfiltration surface. Recorded not-recommended-as-PM-host (forcing residual captured in tests/wrappers/pm-codex/evidence/green-05-web); closing it needs an upstream codex hard-disable or an egress control outside codex (not a Cartopian asset).
mcp_server_note: codex launches the cartopian MCP server (wrappers/etc/mcp-cartopian-only.json command) as a long-lived subprocess at session start, OUTSIDE the per-command Seatbelt/Landlock sandbox that wraps model-generated shell commands. The MCP server therefore retains its own filesystem access and the contained PM stays functional through the Cartopian toolset — exactly as the Claude Code floor relies on out-of-process MCP access.
